summary: LocalOrders Phase 3 (Order Management) backend tested end-to-end via the public ingress URL with a fresh 26-case pytest suite. ALL 26/26 PASS. Full regression across Phase 1 + Phase 2 + Phase 3 = 76/76 PASS. Coverage: Accept (Requested→Accepted, acceptedDateTime set, second accept→400, buyer→403, non-owner seller→403); Reject (empty/whitespace/missing reason all→422, success sets rejectedDateTime+rejectionReason, blocked after Accepted, buyer→403); Deliver (only Accepted, blocked from Requested/Rejected/Delivered, deliveredDateTime set, buyer→403, non-owner seller→403); Cancel (from Requested with no reason → cancellationReason=null, from Accepted with reason → reason stored, blocked from Delivered/Rejected/Expired, seller→403, non-owner buyer→403); 24h auto-expiry verified by directly mutating requestedDateTime in Mongo then re-reading via /seller/orders, /buyer/orders, and /orders/{id} — all three lazy-flip status='Expired' and populate expiredDateTime; once Expired, accept/reject/deliver/cancel all return 400. /api/orders/{id} response contains every new field (acceptedDateTime, rejectedDateTime, cancelledDateTime, deliveredDateTime, expiredDateTime, rejectionReason, cancellationReason). seller_summary exposes mobileNumber (click-to-call) on BOTH /api/buyer/connections and /api/orders/{id} counterparty; buyer_summary exposes mobileNumber on seller-side detail. Zero Mongo _id leakage in any Phase 3 response (recursive scanner verified).

minor.issue: Role/ownership check ordering: ensure_seller/ensure_buyer runs first (returns 403 for wrong role) BEFORE _transition_order which would also have returned 403 for non-owner. Both spec'd behaviours (cross-role 403 and cross-owner 403) verified — informational only.

Legacy Phase-2 constant still lists ['Requested','Accepted','Rejected','Fulfilled','Cancelled'] — missing 'Delivered' and 'Expired'. Unused at runtime (no validator references it) so no functional impact, but stale. Suggest update to ['Requested','Accepted','Rejected','Cancelled','Delivered','Expired'].

action_items: No functional fixes required for Phase 3 acceptance — every spec behaviour is verified green.
Optional cleanup: update ORDER_STATUSES constant in server.py to reflect the new Phase 3 status set (remove 'Fulfilled', add 'Delivered' and 'Expired').
Optional polish: when ready for scale, consider splitting server.py (now ~900 lines) into routers/{auth,inventory,connections,orders}.py and a shared models.py.
critical_code_review_comments: Lazy expiry uses ISO-string lt comparison on requestedDateTime — works because all timestamps are stored in UTC ISO-8601 with the same offset suffix. Verified by aging an order to -25h and re-reading via three different endpoints.

expire_stale_orders(order_id=...) does a targeted update_many filtered by Status='Requested' and the 24h cutoff — race-safe, idempotent. Detail endpoint correctly passes order_id so other unrelated stale orders aren't touched on a single read.

_transition_order centralises ownership + status guard + update — DRY. role check happens via ensure_seller/ensure_buyer before the function runs so wrong-role callers consistently get 403, then non-owner role-matched callers also get 403 inside _transition_order.

RejectOrderRequest.reason validator strips and rejects empty/whitespace, producing FastAPI 422 — exactly as required. CancelOrderRequest.reason is Optional and the route normalises whitespace-only to None before writing.

order_public() projects exactly the contracted public surface; counterparty is built via seller_summary/buyer_summary (already excludes pinHash/failedAttempts/lockedUntil). Recursive _id scanner across every Phase 3 response confirmed zero leakage.

seller_summary already included mobileNumber from Phase 2 — click-to-call requirement is satisfied without any change; tests positively assert presence + value match in both /buyer/connections and /orders/{id}.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seeds. Each test class self-registers fresh buyers/sellers via random 10-digit mobiles and uses a fresh make_connected_pair() helper. The 24h-expiry tests directly mutate orders.requestedDateTime in MongoDB to -25h then re-read via the API to trigger lazy expiry.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase3_order_management.py. Reuses pattern from Phase 2 (random mobile registration + asyncio.run(_age_*) for time-travel). Helpers: make_connected_pair(s) returns a dict with b_token/b_user/s_token/s_user/item; place_order(s, cp, qty=2.0) places an order. Combined regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` → 76 passing in ~41s. MONGO_URL/DB_NAME must remain set in backend/.env for the expiry tests to age orders.
rca_of_issue: N/A — Phase 3 implementation fully matches the spec; no bugs reproduced.
